where tree trunks, several large branches and some mud are piled up in a crude
dam blocking the flow of water, saplings and green plants have sprouted. The dam
is wide and strong enough to provide support for crossing the stream, though
water forced out of its bed has turned surrounding areas into a bog.
name: Flooded Meadow

desc:
Tall willow trees separate a clear water pond from surrounding meadows and
almost completely hide it from view. A small stream supplying the pond with
fresh water gently slides through the plain from north-west. The slight slopes
leading to the pond are covered with animal tracks, and seem to be quite
slippery due to a mud and a wide blanket of old, fallen leaves. Bottom of the
pond is covered with green and brown weeds growing on underwater flat stones,
providing good hiding spots for smaller fish.
name: Trampled Grass by a Pond

desc:
A massive angular boulder of the size of a small house is resting in the high
grass. Soft and moist green-coloured moss covers the boulder at the northern
side while at the south side the plain time-worn rock is exposed. Natural steps
formed on the eroded surface lead up to the top like a stairway. The ground near
the boulder is overgrown with bramble and honeysuckle, giving way to green,
rolling grasslands.
name: A Huge Boulder in the Grass

desc:
The fields are thickly covered with clover, which flourishes in the rich,
fertile soil. Only occasional patches of lush, green grass and few higher bushes
are mixed in, whereas white and red clover dominate everywhere else. In the
summertime when the clover blooms, bees and other insects are attracted by the
sweet-smelling nectar dripping from small flower-heads, but when the flowers
wither, the insects also disappear.
name: Clover Fields

desc:
The soil here is moist and fertile, giving life to long, lush grass and
occasional tall trees towering above the plains. The way east opens up to a deep
quarry, with sheer unclimbable walls dropping down below, making it too
difficult to traverse. To the south are bushes which are small and infrequent
but begin to get denser, making passage in this direction harder. Intermingled
with the bushes are scorched grasses and burnt twigs; evidence of a fire that
must have raged south of here.
name: Fertile Grassland

desc:
The moist yet sandy soil here is surprisingly fertile, as it gives life to the
lush vegetation. A wild mixture of foot high grass, flowers and rare bushes
covers the land, taking advantage of some underground source of water. To the
south, thick bushes overtake the terrain, blocking the view.
name: Fertile Grassland

desc:
The sea-like grasslands sway to and fro in the breeze. Sparsely growing clusters
of bushes stand here and there, barely rising over the long, hair-like blades of
grass. To the north, the ground slowly elevates towards a small hill.
name: Wide Plains

desc:
Thriving vegetation grows here due to the proximity of the small spring that
bubbles out clear, cold water, excellent for drinking. Tracks of wild animals
cover the wet, grass-covered soil around the water. Wide grassy plains extend to
the horizon in the east.
name: Spring in the Grasslands

desc:
A tangle of thorny bushes interlaced by dry yellow grass makes this area barely
passable, yet gives plenty of cover to the local fauna. Small patches, clear of
brush, are blooming with flowers. To the south, thick brush continues, although
not as dense. The brush is too thick to the west to pass through.
name: Dry Brushland

desc:
A worn path cuts through the grasslands from the west to the east. As it
approaches the stone wall of an ruined, fortified farm, the path turns to the
south and runs alongside the wall for a while. Sitting atop a high cliff, the
building's stone walls appear higher than they are. To the east, north, and
south the grasslands continue, dotted with numerous trees and small hills.
name: Path by the Stone Wall

desc:
The faint grassy trail bends around a dried pine tree, heading north and east.
The sides of the trail are covered with small tangled shrubs, not high enough to
hinder the view. In the near distance the trail disappears from sight, almost
indistinguishable from the wide plains stretching to all sides. The grasslands,
dotted with numerous trees, slightly slope downward toward the west.
name: Bend in a Faint Trail

desc:
A faint, grassy trail runs over a small hill and continues to the west and
south. The gently rising slopes are thickly clothed with green bushes, the only
visible passage being the narrow trail. The western slope is covered with soft,
thick grass and filled with pitfalls, making it hard to travel with any wheeled
vehicle. Thorny shrubs guarding the trail are reaching out, trying to grab weary
travellers.
name: A Faint Trail over a Hill

desc:
The rich vegetation west and north of here is gradually replaced with
brown-stained low grass. Interspersed among the grass are patches of small, dry
bushes and weeds. Most plants have given up the fight to survive in the barren
soil consisting mainly of dry sand, with the remaining cutting weeds fighting
for every little drop of water. The barren ground continues to the east.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Long, fertile grass grows everywhere. The tall stems grow in some places higher
than a large man. A long-dead tree stands here, and in its rotten trunk a bird
seems to have made a nest.
name: In the Grassland

desc:
The grass of the plains grows tall and strong. Green, gold and brown seem to
have been the primary colours on Yavanna's palette. As far as the eye can see
rolling plains stretch in all directions. From time to time, the top layer of
the grass ripples gently on the open plains.
name: Lush Grass

desc:
The lush, verdant grass of the plain surrounds everything here. Hues of green,
brown and gold dominate the view in all directions. Occasional rabbit holes and
mole hills are the only signs of life in this secluded area, the only sound is
the rustling of grass.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The trail winds its way west towards the Anduin and south through rolling
plains. Weeds and grass threaten to take the trail back into natures grasp as
the weeds creep across its edges. The vast plains flow like a sea of green and
gold on each side, the tops of the grass undulating slowly like soft waves.
name: Small Trail

desc:
Only the occasional stones lying along the borders of the trail reveal that this
was once a well-travelled road. Now the trail looks abandoned and has been
invaded by grass that covers the surrounding fields.
name: Turn in a Small Trail

desc:
Small valleys and hillocks slope up and down, lining the plain in an intricate
drawing. In the valleys small downs covered by lush grass extend all around. A
grassy trail lies to the north of the hillocks, and turns to the south east of
here. The plain continues to the west, but to the south a silhouette of a larger
structure resting on top of a low hill is outlined against the green grasslands.
name: Undulating Plain

desc:
The grass has grown wildly over the area. Several parts of the field are filled
with wild cereal, their high ears making the travel a bit difficult, while in
other areas of the pasture lush grass makes it easy and pleasant for a traveller
to walk through. To the north a faint trail cuts the plain from east to west.
The grassland continues in all other directions.
name: Rough Pasture

desc:
The grass is high and thick providing the ideal food for the animals of the
prairie. To the north a trail crosses the plain. To the west the ground rises
gently, while to the east the pasture continues unaltered.
name: Pasture in a Plain

desc:
A gentle slope covered with grass leads to an escarpment rising up to the north.
The grass on this slope is unusually lush and, beneath it, small channels can be
seen to furrow the ground, dug by rain running down the slope over time. Small
holes are barely visible through the thick vegetation underfoot. A green pasture
can be seen to the east, bordered by a small trail. South and west the plain
continues uninterrupted.
name: Gently Rising Plain

desc:
The barren land here is unnaturally flat, with only a few mottled trees towards
the north, bordering the hill in that direction. The soil must have lost its
fertility, as absolutely nothing grows within its bounds and the few trees that
stand have no life flowing through their bark.
name: Ruined Farmyard

desc:
The ground is unnaturally flat here, one can assume that someone took the time
to level the terrain. The soil is loose and moist, perhaps because of the
northern hill that partly shades these grounds. Although nothing is growing from
the ground now, a razed house to the west gives way to the notion that this and
the surrounding areas were once a small farming settlement.
name: Old Garden

desc:
Wide patches of tall, reed-like grasses rise above the yellow coloured sandy
ground, obscuring much of the view. The same kind of high grass can be seen to
the south but the grass seems to open up both to the east and west. Here and
there tiny trees and bushes struggle to survive.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Long grasses are growing all over the plains here, drooping over from their own
weight. A number of small evergreen bushes dot the gentle slope to the east,
where a small hill rises over the horizon.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
A flat hill barely rises amid the expanse of plains that stretch in every
direction. Gold and green coloured bushes grow bountifully on the western slope,
leaving few free spots for grass. To the east, a few miniature trees can be
seen: a rare sight in this area.
name: Small Hill

desc:
Several midget-sized trees barely rise over the grass covered landscape. To the
west the ground rises slowly, coming to a peak at the top of the small knoll.
Reaching the horizon to the south and east, the terrain looks monotonous, giving
nothing to focus on.
name: Small Elevation

desc:
A sea of tall, lush grass which waves slighty with wind covers the ground
completely. Small white flowers that reach out above the grass to catch every
last sunray add colour and variety to the plains. Judging by the noises and
small holes in the soil, the place is rich with small animals to which the lush
grass is the perfect hideout.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A small pond is hidden in a thick grove of birches. The sandy bottom of the pond
is clearly visible through the clear water and only a narrow strip of firm wet
sand separates it from the trees. In contrast to the crippled trees in
surrounding grasslands, the birches here look strong and healthy, and a lot of
different wildflowers fringe the forest floor around their trunks.
name: A Grove of Birches

desc:
The walls of the farm in the east tower above the rolling plains, which blend
with the horizon far away in a blue haze. Just next to the wall lies an old, dry
pond that was once filled with deep water and probabaly fishes, but now only
high grasses and thorny brambles flourish in the shade provided by the walled
farm. Small weeds have even started to grow between the stones high up at the
wall, as if trying to climb over to the other side.
name: Rolling Plains by the Stone Wall

desc:
From the top of this small hill, a tiny trail can be seen snaking from the north
into the southern forest. The hill gradually descends to the west, until it
meets the majestic Anduin. The healthy grass that covers this knoll rolls
outwards to the east, creating a green carpet that fades out of view.
name: Top of a Hill

desc:
The plain comes to an abrupt end here. After climbing in a gentle slope from the
south, the grassland sinks to the north in a bush-covered escarpment. The bushes
lining the top of the escarpment are too dense to move through; they rise to no
great height, but their branches are interwoven to form a continuous, compact
tangle. Low below to the north and to the east a trail can be seen, coming from
the west.
name: Escarpment along a Trail

desc:
Sloping down in all directions, this grassy but rocky hill gives a panoramic
view over the areas nearby. The Anduin can be seen far to the west, flowing from
the north to the south through vast grasslands. The thick grass continues
upwards to the east, blocking the view in that direction.
name: Grassy Hillock

desc:
In its travel across the plains, the trail turns here once more to go round a
steep escarpment that raises to the west. Green pastures grow south while the
trail continues to the east bordered by a deep ditch.
name: Bend in a Trail

desc:
This trail is faint and almost completely covered by grass. A deep muddy ditch
borders the road to the north. The trail continues to the east and to the west.
To the south the sea of grass continues endlessly.
name: Track in a Plain

desc:
The trail comes from the banks of the river to the west and continues east
towards Mirkwood. Possibly once used by traders, now the road is completely
covered by grass and can hardly be discerned among the plain. The presence of a
ditch to the north is the only evidence that people once worked in making this
track.
name: Grassy Trail

desc:
The trail is faint and hardly recognisable among the grass. The trail leads from
the west and turns to the south here. It is bordered by a sparse line of trees
to the north. To the east the ground is plain and grassy.
name: Bend in a Grassy Trail

desc:
Man-high grass stretches in all directions in a sea of gold, green and brown.
The slightly hypnotic nature of the undulating fronds and the calming hiss of
the stems lightly brushing against each other, distracts from the fact this
place is empty and isolated.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
The grass is thinner here and well-trodden. A trampled path leads northwards
towards the ruins of an old building. The building looks quite derelict and the
upper levels look to have fallen long ago, most of the wooden frames around the
doors and windows look rotten. It appears that there are current inhabitants as
there is a faint wisp of smoke coming from the inside. There is a wooden gate to
the east that looks to be some kind of animal pen.
name: A Dip in a Plain

desc:
The ground in this enclose is worn and only has small tufts of grass growing on
the otherwise bare earth. There is a small trough half-filled with stagnant
water. A pile of hay has been heaped in the corner, whether its use is as a
bedding place for animals or as a compost is not obvious, for the ground here is
covered in animal droppings. The only exit from this area is the gate to the
west.
name: An Enclosure

desc:
Aquatic plants grow in abundance both in the water and around the edge of this
small pond. The water looks cool and clean. Animal tracks lead from it in every
direction. A hill to the north is visible above the swaying grass.
name: A Small Pond

desc:
From the top of this small hill the surrounding landscape can be seen spreading
for miles all around. Mostly, the area is dominated by vast plains which extend
to all directions like a huge, lush, verdant sea. To the north, a dense thicket
stands in the middle of the otherwise open area.
name: A Hill in the Plains

desc:
Tall grasses grow densely together here, creating an almost impenetrable wall of
green- and brown-coloured vegetation. Newer grasses entwine themselves around
the older ones, creating a maze-like effect and making it easy to lose sense of
direction in this entangled labyrinth. Although giving an appearance of a soft
and rolling blanket, the stems are tough and sharp, blocking all travelling to
the south.
name: Entangled Plain

desc:
The lush, green plains dominate the area continuing as gently flowing grass far
to the west, and as bushy, tangled landscape to the east. To the north the
ground is more rocky, and the plains descends down between boulders and small
rocks, transforming into a narrow canyon.
name: Entrance to a Narrow Canyon

desc:
The plains flow in all directions here, lush green grass grows amongst the dry
browns of last season. Thorny weeds grow amongst dainty flowers, concealing the
dangerous thorns. An old nest lies here amongst the stems presumably from some
small rodent long since deceased.
name: Quiet Plain

desc:
A line of sparse trees borders the northern side of the trail. A steep rise
leads to the plains north. To the west the ground sinks into a muddy ditch,
while to the east lies the vast plain.
name: Scattered Line of Trees

desc:
A tall, verdant sea of grass sways gently on the plains coloured in all shades
of green and gold. It is difficult to see in any direction as the grass stands
so tall, but to the west the plain is cut by a steep and narrow canyon, and to
the east the tall grass is changing into an impenetrable shrubbery.
name: Tangled Plain

desc:
The trail comes from the west and turns here to the south. Turfs and debris
cover the ground making the trail hardly visible, but it can be still travelled
over easily. To the north, a thick group of trees is preventing a clear view to
the plains behind. To the east a deep ditch runs along the road.
name: Trail in a Plain

desc:
The trail makes a sharp bend here to go around a high escarpment that prevents
travel to the south and west. Bushes cover the steep slope of the escarpment and
nearly block the trail itself. High earth walls surround the path to the north,
blocking the view after a short distance. The uneven ground shows that the road
is not often used. To the east the plain lowers and the trail seems to
disappear, covered by the vegetation.
name: Sharp Bend in a Deeply Cut Trail

desc:
The trail cuts the plain like a deep wound. The track is easy to follow, even in
places that show disuse. The path comes from west and here it turns southwards.
To the north the slope is too steep to climb, while to the east it seems
possible to reach the plain above.
name: Turn of a Deeply Cut Trail

desc:
Although it appears to have not been used in ages, this overgrown trail is easy
to follow as its texture is quite different than the plains that it cuts
through. It was probably once a very basic supply route, leading from the river
Anduin, however it has not seen such use in quite some time. The brush on the
south side of the trail is very dense, thwarting travel in that direction.
name: Bend in a Trail

desc:
Thin, green brush covers the heightened landscape here, almost completely
shielding the ground from view. The brush becomes denser as the field slopes
down towards the east and north, rendering travel in those directions
impossible.
name: Light Brush

desc:
A group of trees grows along a trail giving some shelter from unwanted
observers. The trail is covered by sand and gravel, and occasional tufts of
grass and weed grow on it, indicating that this path is rarely used. The field
around the road is covered by green, lush grass.
name: Trees Bordering a Trail

desc:
The plain slopes toward the south, ending in a ditch which borders what once
could have been a road. To the north a dense patch of shrubs can be seen. To the
west the plain is dotted by a group of trees and to the east a thorny hedge
crosses the plain.
name: Gentle Slope

desc:
The terrain elevates steadily from a mossy ditch located to the south, through
the peaceful and grassy plain, towards a wall of thorny brush to the north. The
plains continue to the west. The thick, long wall of brush seems to prevent the
travelling to the north. To the east, a steep and unstable slope can be seen.
name: Below a Thorny Hedge

desc:
Short, dense bushes and rolling, dry grass cover the plains. The only signs of
living creatures are tracks on the ground and some holes among the grass. Far to
the west the bank of the Anduin is bordered by some trees. To the north a steep
hill blocks the way, while plains continue to all the other directions.
name: In the Plains

desc:
The ground here is hard and the grass is dry. The benign influence of the Anduin
does not reach this part of the plain. The signs of animals are rare and hard to
find. To the west and north the vegetation is lusher. To the east and south the
plain becomes barren.
name: Sparse Plain

desc:
There is very little that would be of interest here. The grass is shorter, but
it still looks basically the same as the rest of the vegetation in the plains.
There are a few dull, dark grey rocks laying around.
name: Empty Plains

desc:
Tall stalks of grass wave in the wind, slightly browned by the sun that usually
beats down upon the plains. To the north lies an ancient section of wall built
by whatever peoples inhabited this land long ago. To the east and west the
plains continue.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
In sharp contrast to the surrounding grass, this bit of earth is completely
bare. Some kind of trampling or some foul event has removed all the vegetation
leaving the soil hard and smooth.
name: Dry Area in the Plains

desc:
The faint path coming from the west, turns here to the north and sinks in a bowl
of grass that lies below the plain. To the south another gorge opens in the
plain, showing that this part of the land is rich of springs and water.
name: Bend in a Vanishing Path

desc:
The sides of this deep gorge, although rising high at a gentle rate, are almost
circular in shape, giving the appearance of a bowl. The tall grass of the plains
can be seen swaying all around the tops of the gully save to the south where an
impenetrable-looking thicket grows. Rocks and stones litter the floor. Clearly
visible near the centre of the ground is a strange dark hole; a faint echo of
water dripping can be heard emanating from its dark depths.
name: Giant Kettle in a Wide Gorge

desc:
Rocks and boulders have fallen down from the steep faces of a gully, making the
visibility to be very limited in this narrow passage; little is visible ahead as
the huge boulders block the sight. What is still visible is the grass from the
plains, as it sways gently far above, on the edges of the gully. The sides of
the gorge look too steep to climb though, so it seems to be a choice of either
pressing on or turning back.
name: A Narrow Gully

desc:
The ground here is bare and with no sign of living creatures. The vegetation
grows thriving in all other directions. A tall hedge can be seen to the west. To
the south a slope of sliding terrain ends at some trees.
name: Bare Terrain

desc:
Every direction, as far as the eye can see, is coloured with different shades of
green and gold. The main colour is green, but occasional bushes of flowers are
visible trough the grass, struggling to survive among the weeds and grasses.
name: Amidst the Tall Grasses

desc:
The monotony of the sea of grass is interrupted by a tall hedge of thorny bushes
that block the way to the south. Travel can be continued to the east, north or
west.
name: Thorny Hedge in a Plain

desc:
Some willow trees grow here, along the bank of Anduin the Great that flows near
to the west, giving moderate shelter among these vast grassy plains. The trees
stretch their roots toward the water and little leaves dance in the air as they
fall, carried by the breeze, to the ground or water. The ground is soft, rich
and covered by leaves. To the north and east the plains continue as far as the
eye can see. Southwest is a trail coming from the south and heading west to the
river.
name: Cluster of Willows

desc:
A few groups of trees, namely willows and pines, are spotted in this field that
overlooks the majestic Anduin River. The steep cliffs to the west and north
prevent one from entering and exiting the Great River, as it clashes with the
rock and speeds its way southwards. A huge boulder stands here, the only rock
that can be seen for miles. The ground around appears to have been trodden
recently, allowing the notion that it may be a marker of some sort.
name: Boulder Overlooking the Anduin

desc:
This elevated, steep-sloped hill provides an excellent view of the Anduin River
as it flows from the north and through the grassy plains to the west. Far in the
distance, the Misty Mountains stand tall in the sky with their sharp peaks
resembling huge, pointed teeth growing out of the ground.
name: Grassy Mound above the Great River

desc:
A wide, round swamp lies here, surrounded by reeds. The water supplying this
swamp appears to flow somewhere from the north, bubbling through some large
rocks. The reeds growing in the swamp thrive on the marshy ground. The water-
soaked ground gives the lush grasslands to the west their supply of water. The
overflow of water in this area creates makes the swamp continue to the east.
name: Marshy Field

desc:
The soggy ground here gives an almost swampy feeling in some parts. Water floods
in from the north. There are a few reeds growing here but mostly lush, green
grass blades. The ground begins to become dry further to the south.
name: Wet Fields

desc:
Dark-green grass, brown reeds and low bushes grow here, supplied by the fertile,
wet ground. Narrow, muddy path borders the swamp, winding and wheeling around
the deep water puddles that occupy the ground in several places. The ground is
impassable to the north, gets more wet to the east, while to the south it seems
to slope upwards.
name: Muddy, Wet Plains

desc:
These wetlands are populated with reeds of many types. The ground is covered
with large, deceptive-looking pools, which produce occasional bubbles to the
sticky surface of water. Around the pools, the land is saturated with mud and
moisture. There is a patch of irises growing toward the west. Far beyond the
bog, at the edge of the wetlands to the north and east, thick brush meets the
reeds.
name: Treacherous Wetlands

desc:
A hillock rises amid the plains which stretch for miles in every direction. The
elevated area provides a good view of the surroundings, but the huge granite
boulders lying everywhere allow one to remain hidden at the same time. The grass
on this hillock looks dried and yellow in some places, but in the shade of the
boulders it is lush and healthy, interspersed with some colourful wildflowers.
name: Granite Boulders on a Rocky Hillock

desc:
The tall, thick grass makes rustling sounds and sways gently back and forth when
the winds blow through here. Ranging three or four feet at places, it is long
enough to hide any smaller animals from predators. Plains continue in all sides
except in the east, where a low, grassy hill breaks up the monotony of the
landscape.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The plains abruptly cut off as they approach from the north-west here. Very
little grows in this deep gorge. It seems to be either an old dried up river bed
or perhaps an underground stream that flowed underneath the soil that has
collapsed. Rocks litter the ground, making passage rough and awkward even for
the most sure-footed travellers.
name: Bottom of a Steep Gully

desc:
The canyon is steep and narrow. The walls are rocky and wet. Some debris and
little boulders cover the ground making it unstable to walk across. The canyon
widens to the north into a deep gully, while to the south the ground climbs to a
flat area. The east and west walls are too steep to climb.
name: Narrow Canyon

desc:
Everywhere around this area the tall grass, spiky bushes and rocks of various
sizes seem to knot into a messy network of snarled passages and dead-ends, each
one more perplexing than the previous. So narrow are the paths amidst the
vegetation, that riding is not possible here; it almost seems that the tangle of
swaying bushes twists into a web of confusion just to thwart the traveller's
passage. To the east the ground sinks abruptly in an unclimbable canyon.
name: Intricate Plains

desc:
The top layer of soil is sliding southwards making this area hard to travel
through and impossible to ride. To the east and west the plain is covered by
grass. North of here there is no sign of vegetation. Some trees grow to the
south.
name: Sliding Escarpment

desc:
A deep ditch lies on the border of the plain and a faint trail which runs
through the area in west-east direction. The ground is muddy and covered by
crawling plants. To the north a gentle slope rises towards a more solid soil
covered by grass and bushes.
name: Ditch along a Trail

desc:
The mud here is deep and travelling is hard. Both sides of the ditch are steep
and slippery. The ditch continues to the west, while to the east it disappears
into a line of trees. Climbing the north side of the ditch will lead to a hedge
that crosses the grassy plain, while the southern side of the ditch, too steep
and muddy for one to climb up, borders a faint trail.
name: Muddy Ditch

desc:
The grass here is overgrown by dense shrubs and bushes. Mounts which travellers
may have brought through must be led by hand in this tangle of branches and
thorns. The plain extends in all directions from here.
name: Patch of Shrubs

desc:
Dense brush flourishes on the riverbank here, growing well due to the Anduin
flowing directly east of it. The land here slopes downwards suddenly to the
north, leading to what looks like a small valley. The loud sound of rushing
water is heard to the east, as the Anduin flows along its journey.
name: Bushy Riverbank

desc:
This muddy riverside offers stark contrast to the green plains behind it. The
great River flows on the eastern and southern end of the plain, yielding a muddy
riverbank that is dotted with what seems to be the footprints of small animals
that have traversed here to drink from the river.
name: Muddy Riverside

desc:
Light-green grass covers this mostly flat plain. In some areas the grass is
quite tall, suggesting that the soil that lies underneath it is very rich. The
plains slope downwards to the south and east, where the great river can be seen
winding its way through the vast landscape.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The field here rises upwards ever so slightly, offering a full view of the bushy
riverbank along the southern side, that borders the mighty Anduin. The southern
bank leads steeply into the river, forcing those who might wish to embark into
it to search elsewhere.
name: Rising Plains near the River Anduin

desc:
Rolling over the plains and meadows, the carpet of lush grass here adds a soft
perspective to this field. The ground is hardly flat, but it is not harsh or
ragged, providing a very gentle grassland that is reminiscent of a feeding
pasture found on farms for livestock. What appears to be an old streambed lies
to the west, one could jump down the bank, but probably not climb back into this
pasture from it.
name: Pasture in the Plains

desc:
With the Anduin River bordering the plains on two sides, this grassy field seems
quite fertile. The two banks bordering the river offer a contrast to each other.
The southern bank is predominately rocky, and seemingly very hazardous, while
the eastern bank has thick, waist-high brush blocking entry to the Great River.
name: Plains near the Great River

desc:
A small pond of calm, dark water has placed its bed in the ground here. Its
shape is reminiscent of an eye, not exactly rounded, but more of an oval,
pinching at the east and west sides. Because of its small size, the edges of the
pond rise higher than the grassy ground surrounding it.
name: The Water Eye Pond

desc:
Many rocks are strewn about the riverbank here, most of them small in size and
of light colours. The Anduin splashes noisily over them, causing them to be
quite slippery and making traversing to the river possible, but unsafe. The
ground beyond the riverbank is grassy, with full shrubs protruding from the
fertile soil.
name: Rocky Riverbank

desc:
Many rocks and stones litter the plain here, perhaps cast here after being
slowly moved through the Great River that flows immediately to the south. Many
of the rocks and stones are tiny, however, a few of them are quite large and
have been smoothed by the river flowing over them for many years.
name: Rocky Plain near the Great River

desc:
Thin brush grows all over this field, with its green foliage rising and dipping
throughout the area giving the landscape a very textured look. The pointed
leaves adoring the branches serve as a defence from grazing wildlife, however,
many smaller animals could seek refuge from predators under the brush.
name: Light Brush

desc:
With the River Anduin directly to the east and south, this low bank is never
devoid of water and has allowed vast amounts of brush to grow all about the
riverbank. While most of the brush is quite harmless, there are some varieties
that stand almost a yard high that make navigation among this riverside slightly
difficult.
name: Brushy Riverside

desc:
The plains here rise from the east and south, yielding a view of the majestic
Anduin as it snakes it way through the plains to the south. Thin, soft grasses
cover the fertile plains, giving the notion that the environment here is not
very harsh during any of the seasons.
name: Elevated Plains

desc:
The grassy field here is almost unnaturally flat, with most of the terrain near
it rising away from here. Passage to the north is blocked by an old stone wall
that is crumbling from years of decay and what appears to be hasty, cheap work.
To the south and east the grassy plains rise slightly upwards, keeping the far
view of what may lay beyond them. In the middle of the field on the western
side, an old dilapidated hut barely stands.
name: Flat Plain

desc:
This small hut would fall in upon itself, were there not a few sturdy log
columns, which seem to be placed here after the original construction work, to
keep the roof standing. This hut was probably constructed long time ago by
travellers following the Anduin, to seek shelter from the rain. Judging from the
lack of any furnishing, it was meant to be a transient dwelling. The dust on the
floor is marked with fresh footprints that seem to stop in the middle of the
room.
name: Dilapidated Hut

desc:
The parallel walls of this cavern are lined with crude bunks, with no apparent
care for organization or neatness. Against the far wall, stand many weapon and
armour racks, in close reach of the inhabitants of this filthy barracks. The
floor is covered in dust and dirt, and in a far corner many bloody blades lay in
disarray.
name: Barracks

desc:
Lots and lots of brush dominate the plains in every direction, having pushed the
grass away by sucking the nutrients from the soil and blocking the sun from
enriching the grass with its large, pointed leaves. The ground here is slightly
lower than the surrounding plains, but one can not tell that just by looking
around, as the brush stands taller than the surroundings.
name: Brush-covered Plains

desc:
Thin brush and lush grasses cover the plains here, adding a myriad of colours
and textures to the landscape. The rich, soft grass flows endlessly here,
contrasted by the rough, prickly brush that dots the plains. The plain tilts
upwards to the west, where a small hill lies.
name: Slanted Plains

desc:
Rising from the plains, this low hill has very little plant life on its crown.
There are tufts of grasses littered about the bases of the hill, but gradually
they become less common towards the crest of the hill. While the hill stands
above its surroundings, it is not quite tall enough to command a pronounced view
of the area. The way south is blocked by a crumbling stone wall.
name: Withered Hill

desc:
A grassy slope gently rises from here, leading to the south. The grass on the
slope is quite lush, fed by the rain water that drips down the hill before being
absorbed into the topsoil. The bottom of the slope is dotted with various shrubs
and brush that has found solace in the fertile soil.
name: Gently Rising Plain

desc:
Suddenly rising from the grasslands, a large, jagged rock stands about three
yards high here. Its sides are grey and rough, with the bottom half mostly
covered by a thick, brown moss. The grass covers the field completely, with the
giant rock being the only object blocking its flow.
name: Rock in the Grasslands

desc:
The plain gently rises here, yielding a view east and north over the remains of
a dried stream. The grass on the plain here is very lush, contrasting the brown
that can be seen in the riverbed. Travel to the east is made more difficult by a
ledge that lowers into the streambed.
name: Plain Overlooking a Dried Riverbed

desc:
A thin strip of hardened mud streaks through the green grass here, arriving from
the northern end of the plain and continuing south to what appears to be a pond.
The streambed, while not very large, is lower than its surrounding ground
offering two very distinct terrain types to the plains. A high bank halts
anything that wishes to proceed east.
name: Dried Streambed

desc:
The plains extends in all directions, void of any hills and dips, completely
flat. Thin, short grass covers the ground, flowing smoothly over the plains. The
grass reaches out in every direction, connecting the vast fields together.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
Scattered shrubs dot the field, and dissipate towards this field, which is
mostly covered in grass, with a few barren plots of a hard, brown soil
interrupting the ground. To the north, a steep hill rises from the ground
blocking passage in that direction.
name: South of a Hill

desc:
Slanting downwards from the west, and continuing to the east, this plain is made
up of caked, brown soil. Hard and desolate, nothing is able to grow in the
immediate area. Grass lightly creeps in from the north, starting from the ledge
which prevents travel in that direction.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
The remains of what once used to be a small stream cuts through this field, its
low path and hardened soil brings a solemn mood to the area that goes against
the playful scene of lush, green meadows that surround it. Steep ledges on the
north and east banks prevent travel, and outline the path the stream once
followed.
name: Dried Streambed

desc:
Various grasses extend to the far corners of this field. They range from a short
and dark grass, to a taller, lighter variety. The field does not appear to be
flat, but the terrain can not be guessed for certain, as the taller grass could
be hiding many small dips and rises. A brush-covered hill towers above the field
on the east side.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
The blots of sand and grass change repeatedly over the terrain, making the
landscape more interesting with various colours. To the north the foliage
becomes more dense, and behind the small bushes the shape of a small sparse
forest can be made out. Far in the west, the peaks of Misty Mountains rise as a
wall between these wide plains and the western land.
name: Sandy Field

desc:
The grasslands flatten out here, opening up a wide view of the surrounding
fields. Most of the grasses on this plain are quite dense, providing many small
nooks and thickets for the various indigenous rodents to claim a haven. A steep
bank prevents travel to the dried old streambed which lies to the west.
name: Vast Plain

desc:
A light sand covers most of the terrain here, along with patches of rich soil
and lush green grass. Giving a more interesting appearance to this field, small
sinkholes are formed within the sand, making the terrain taxing to traverse.
name: Sandy Field

desc:
The land here intertwines with its surroundings very well, making it hard to
tell just how far, or how long one has travelled in this meadow. A few different
types of grasses cover the meadow, the most abundant being a thin bladed, brown
grass. A good-sized cliff borders the southern part of the meadow, leading to
what appears to be a dried up stream.
name: Vast Meadow

desc:
Dipping downwards from the west, the land here becomes increasingly wet,
suggesting that this slight dip holds rainwater for a bit longer than its more
elevated neighbouring plains. The ground is covered with scattered shrubbery and
grass is seen growing in the wet soil that makes up this miniature swamp.
name: Wetlands

desc:
Standing alone above the plains, this small hill rises from the ground, offering
a unique view of the encircling lands not possible from the lower fields and
meadows. Rising smoothly from the north and east, travel is cut off to the south
by the steep slope in that direction. While the western side is steep, the brush
would make great footholds for climbing down.
name: Grassy Knoll

desc:
The river begins to spread out and become slightly more shallow as it bends from
the west and heads south. There are many pebbles covering the river bed here,
visible under the calmer parts of the river.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The river seems deeper here as it is squeezed by its banks on either side. The
steep bank to the west drops off so sharply that passage upward is impossible.
To the east a riverbank can be seen with some poles standing out of the water.
The current of the river flows quickly here, causing small rapids to show on the
surface of the river.
name: In the Anduin

desc:
As the great river makes the turn here, from the west towards the south, the
river seems to narrow some, causing the depth to increase. The eastern bank is
covered with reeds and a few irises, thriving on the low lying riverbank
environment. The western bank towers above the river toward the southwest.
name: Bend in the River Anduin

desc:
A large tree, fallen from the forested riverbank to the west, lies across the
river here, branches dragging against the water. The river runs at a fast pace
here, bending to the east as it continues its way south.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The Anduin river flows faster in this part than elsewhere. The water is so fast
that it forms many white-water eddies. To the east, reeds and irises grow in the
shallow part of the river. The increased flow drags them down, making them sway
like darting vipers with the current of the river's flow.
name: River Anduin

desc:
The river flows rapidly downstream towards the south. There is a steep riverbank
to the west. On the eastern side of the river there is a shallow, calm pool. The
riverbank to the east seems to slope down and come close to the elevation of the
water's surface here. The river seems to increase in speed as it flows south,
and rapids can be seen cutting the surface of the water at certain places.
name: River Anduin

desc:
The water flows at a decent pace here, but the rapids are not so fast that one
could not see the bottom of the river. There are well polished rocks covering
the riverbed. To the west the riverbank looms above the river. To the east the
bank is steeper coming from the north and angles to a lower elevation before
continuing south.
name: River Anduin

desc:
Water flows briskly downriver towards the south. Both riverbanks are fairly
steep on either side, but the western side is steeper than the eastern one. To
the north the river appears to make a turn from the east. A large, black boulder
sits deep in the water along the western shore.
name: River Anduin

desc:
The great Anduin river flows in from the north and bends to the west as it flows
through this section of the river. Reeds and irises line the southern riverbank.
There is a short drop off to the east where the river meets the meadow.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The river flows calmly to the south after finishing its southwesterly curve. The
water is quite serene here; rocks at the bottom of the river are visible through
the water. To the east, a riverbank lined with reeds reveals a green meadow. To
the west the bank is steep but still climbable.
name: River Anduin

desc:
Flowing in from the north, the river makes a gradual curve toward the southwest.
The low banks on the northwestern and southeastern sides of the river allow
shallow river plants to grow healthily. Reeds and irises conceal the ground
close to the sides of the river. The water moves at a swift pace but has a calm
look to its surface.
name: Curve in the Anduin

desc:
Almost as quickly as the water speeds in from the north, the Great River quickly
turns its way to the west, finding its way between large stones that sit in the
riverbed. White foam and small bubbles develop from the water as it pushes over
the stones hiding just beneath the surface.
name: Great Curve in the Anduin River

desc:
The wide river is flowing from the north to the west here, rushing to carve its
way through the undulating plains. A small hill blocks the view to the east, and
the shore to the south offers a rocky landing spot before the great river turns
towards that direction.
name: The River Anduin

desc:
A small hill blocks the flow of water to the south, so the Great River takes a
turn to the west. A mess of water-rotten wooden logs and smaller branches are
stuck in the overhanging brush that reaches out from the eastern bank.
name: Bend in the Great River

desc:
Clumps of wooden logs clutter the banks of the Anduin here, stuck along the
southern shore, or in the brush on the eastern bank. The river starts to shrink
again as it flows west, speeding up slightly and changing the calm that is
experienced towards the north, where the river was much wider.
name: Driftwood in the Anduin

desc:
The Great River flows here from the east and then changes direction, continuing
on its southern path through the great plains. The northern bank is quite bare,
with the brown mud giving the water rushing along the bank a darker colour.
name: Bend in the Anduin River

desc:
The water is spread between the low riverbanks, giving it a calm demeanour. The
northern riverbank is covered with rocks, behind them the grassy plains drift
away, calmly dropping out of sight. To the west, the Anduin is slightly altering
its course southwards.
name: Great River

desc:
The Great River is making its turn from the east to the south. The curve is
quite placid, with the water surface not showing any signs of disturbances at
the turn. Weeds and small bushes can be seen on the banks to the north and west.
Many stones on the northern bank are textured with white tags, clarifying the
visit of many birds.
name: Curve in the Anduin

desc:
The great river Anduin flows in a southwestern direction as it cuts through the
meadows on either side. The banks of the river on the north and south sides are
quite gradual. Reeds grow at the edge of the river with grass further away from
the river. When the river finishes its curve, it directs itself southbound.
name: Curve in the Anduin

desc:
Here, the river Anduin flows in from the east, continuing its flow to the south.
There is a small, shallow pool collecting at the northwest corner of the bend in
the river, where bits of debris can be seen, trapped among small eddies and
currents. The riverbank on the southeast is lined with reeds.
name: Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The flow of the Anduin is gathering speed here, rushing in from the east and
continuing to the south. Lush plains flow west of here, as far as the eye can
see. The banks on the northern side are inaccessible, with rocks and thick
vegetation making it too dangerous to reach the shore.
name: Bend in the Great River

desc:
The majestic Anduin turns and speeds its way southwards here again, after
crashing into the high banks to the west. The water seems quite deep around this
point, as no rocks are sticking out of the blue surface. The northern and
western riverbanks are not safely accessible as the water rushes over the rocks.
name: Curve in the Anduin

desc:
After coming through a narrow gorge to the east, the mighty Anduin widens here,
allowing the water to calm down while it spreads to wider area. Taking advantage
of the still part of the river, an old log dock is located not much further
downstream the river.
name: The River Anduin

desc:
A huge, blackened wooden dock rises from the eastern bank. Like a clumsy stair,
the logs lie next to one another, ragged and rotten. Still, it can be used as a
comfortable landing place for rafts and boats. Thick brush covers the eastern
bank all around the dock; it is clear that it has not had regular use in many
years. The river is very wide here, allowing it to drift lazily southwards,
perhaps this is why the dock was built at this location.
name: A Ruined Log Dock in the River Anduin

desc:
Continuing through this gorge, the Anduin rushes in from the north, violently
splashing against the walls that make up the banks to the east and south.
Flowing out and to the west, the Anduin River opens up and grows calmer.
name: Sudden Bend in the Great River

desc:
The underground river that once rushed through the caves is long gone, leaving
only smooth rock behind. The cave has a high ceiling which is lowering in the
south, towards the gaping mouth of a tunnel, where a passageway is leading
deeper into the caves. The rock walls have been worked in places making sure
that the path is thoroughly wide enough. Stone steps leading up at the northern
wall end with a rocky door hiding the passage from outside.
name: Entry Cave

desc:
A wide chamber has been skilfully carved to the stone; the work has respected
the earth's form and veins while anyway making room for a large square. A
decorated, bear-shaped fountain stands in the middle of the cave, strangely not
bearing any signs of decay and violence that has obviously raged through this
hall. The walls are decorated with carvings, some of them are still visible
under the dirt, red stains and dust that overwhelms the place now.
name: Fountain Square inside the Stone

desc:
This place has been the living and sleeping area for the inhabitants of this
cave. Now the sleeping alcoves are empty, and the furniture has been razed or
rotten away. Signs of a fierce battle are still visible around the room, as one
can recognise the crude marks left by slashing swords and hitting axes in the
stone. The stone floor is littered with broken dishes, old bones and some
tattered furs.
name: Razed Sleeping Quarters

desc:
The small side chamber is littered with barrels, chests and crates which take up
most of the room here. In the far end corner, crammed in a small free space
between two storage boxes, lies a large pile of old animal furs. It appears that
this cave has not only been used for storage, but as a sleeping chamber as well.
The chamber is separated from the larger open area in the east with an old,
rotten door.
name: Small Storage Chamber

desc:
A narrow, slippery-looking staircase descends along the cavern walls from high
above, directly to a wide underground pool which has occupied this abandoned
cave. The ice cold water is black and calm, disturbed only by an occasional
quiet plop of dripping water which sends tiny ripples across the surface. The
room and the corridor that once existed here are sunken into deep water, but the
passageway continues again in the east where the ground rises from the water.
name: A Sunken Staircase

desc:
Unlike the upper level of this underground stronghold, this skilfully decorated
room shows no signs of destruction done by the time, and no mischief caused by
the enemy. The walls contain small shelves and a few large carvings in the
stone. A massive, arched doorway leads to the east; its broad frames are
uninviting, discouraging to go forward. A row of statues sculpted into forms of
mighty Beornings stand on the both sides of the doorway.
name: The Bear Gate

desc:
An ancient, underground riverbed has been transformed into a simple but
beautiful sanctum, which seems to be abandoned a long time ago. The room is
dominated by a large, smooth rock altar which has probably been carved out from
the bedrock itself. Above the altar, three symbols are visible in the rocky
wall: a huge bear, the river and the moon. Some kind of disaster has hit this
sanctum in the past, as the eastern side has caved in, and a wide crack runs
across the back wall.
name: Ruined Sanctum

desc:
To the east, the river Anduin flows by below the high drop off that makes up the
steep riverbank. A thin coating of brown coloured grass covers the soil of the
field above the river. The bank itself is so steep that it will allow all but a
few weeds to grow upon it. The river flows swiftly but the riverbed is smooth
enough that the surface of the water only breaks occasionally, giving it a
deceptive appearance of gentleness.
name: Field above the Anduin

desc:
The riverbank begins high above the Anduin in the northern area of this field
but it quickly decreases in elevation to the south, forming a natural ramp down
into the water. The eastern side of this riverbank is still quite high, but it
would be no problem to enter the water.
name: Sloping Riverbank

desc:
Healthy, green grass is growing all around and some rocks can be seen rising
here and there among the grasslands. It is clear these lands have seen their
share of wild animals as tracks have formed twisting and turning in the dirt
amidst the rocks. The sound of flowing water can be heard to the east.
name: Green Grasslands

desc:
The yellow-green grass that grows here has been trampled in places as if animals
swept through this area in herds. The rocky soil is only able to support growth
as a result of the abundance of fresh water available from the river Anduin east
of here. Thus otherwise barren grassland offers little or no shelter from any
kind of weather. Only a few sporadic trees bearing short leaves can be found.
name: A Rocky Pasture

desc:
Wild, flowing grass has taken over this whole area and, in place, reaches as
high as a horse stands severely limiting the view of the surrounding lands. The
ground slopes gently towards the river Anduin to the east where it meets the
muddy banks of the river and the grass gives way to softly waving reeds. The
strong current and muddy banks make entering the river at this point too
hazardous.
name: Horse-high Grass

desc:
A small knoll rises above the moist, high grass offering a good view of the
surrounding lands from the muddy ditch sloping into the river in the east to the
grassland that leads away to the north and west. A narrow strip of bulrushes
circle the knoll, their heads waving gently in the breeze.
name: Small Knoll above Anduin

desc:
What was once a rocky riverbank has been covered in a thin layer of fertile silt
from the repeated flooding from the Anduin river that runs alongside. High grass
now grows in the soft, sticky mud but poor drainage means that many swamp-like
patches remain. A few tiny shrubs have sprouted but nothing bigger than that can
grow as it would be impossible for the roots of larger plants to penetrate the
rocky layer just beneath the soil. The riverbank to the east is littered with
sharp rocks and is very steep which would make it too hazardous to enter the
water from here.
name: Moist Grassland

desc:
The ground here is very damp as most likely, the river used to go through these
fields at some point leaving behind a low, flat area that is prone to flooding.
The soil near the riverbank has eroded away making it too steep to safely allow
passage into the river. Tall stands of bulrushes grow in the fertile soil
deposited by the river while grassland stretches away into the distance to the
west and south.
name: Moist Grassland

desc:
High trees grow close together with prominent roots that have erupted from the
ground only to have moss grow over them. Very little sunlight penetrates the
canopy here forcing the branches to grow towards the sky to seek out what little
light is available. To the east, the sounds of water streaming by can be heard,
and from the north, the smell of swamp-mud seeps in. The ground to the north is
very moist and one would have to think twice before venturing there. The forest
to the south seems so dense as to be impenetrable.
name: Border of a Musty Forest

desc:
The flat plains loom high above the river Anduin. The water, in all its power
has worn out the northern bank, which makes it impossible to descend into the
river to the north. The eastern bank is very smooth and steep, which makes it
easy to slide down into the river, but most likely impossible to crawl back up.
name: A High Riverside

desc:
The trees grow wide apart, leaving space for small bushes to grow in the open
areas. In one of the tallest trees, a tall oak, visible high above the ground is
a perfect shelter for woodchucks to sleep during the night. To the north the
trees vanish from sight, and flat land can be seen. The river Anduin flows below
to the east. Its steep bank is covered with broken branches and roots of fallen
trees.
name: In a Forest

desc:
This vast bog is filled with tall swamp plants which rise up from the suckling
pools of moss, mud and water. Plants this high would only grow so tall if they
had lots of nutrients and time. Many of these plants are as thick as an orc's
wrist and tall as small trees, creating deep shadows and blocking the light from
entering the swamp.
name: A Dark Swamp

desc:
The ground is waterlogged, and the soil beneath has turned into viscous,
clinging mud. A horrible stench pollutes the air, and tree-like swamp plants
grow high all around, blocking clean air and most of the sunlight from entering
here. Vines drop down from these high grown plants as if reaching for the watery
ground.
name: A Dark Smelly Swamp

desc:
A deep, muddy ditch runs through the area. Layer upon layer of thick, sticky,
grey mud covers every surface making walking extremely difficult as the much
sucks everything in. A foul smell rises from the stagnant pools of water that
have collected in depressions in the soft mud.
name: Muddy Ditch

desc:
The rapids of the Anduin slam into the bank to the south, carving into the bank,
as the river turns to the east in its travel. The riverbank to the south has
been carved out so much, in fact, that it has a sheer cliff, impossible to
climb.
name: In the Anduin

desc:
This is a vast plain covered with grass. Some gentle slopes make the landscape a
beautiful sight and a pleasant place to travel. No signs of agriculture or other
humanoid activity can be seen. The plain continues in all directions.
name: Vast Plain

desc:
The land here is barren with only small shrubs and low yellowish grass growing
in small tufts. With little to hold the soil together, the erosion from wind has
blown most of it away, leaving only a stony surface. In the east the ground
slopes up to a small hill, whereas in all other directions the endless plains
disappear into the distance.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Covered in mud and decaying vines, the walls of this cavern are dark and cold. A
mix of mud and water covers the ground beneath, making it slimy and slippery. No
light enters the cavern, only this terrible smell sneaks in from somewhere. Some
drag marks can be seen on the ground leading east from here to an opening of
some kind.
name: Muddy Cavern

desc:
This dark hole has mud and rocks on the ground and water dripping from the
ceiling. The walls here are solid, moist and cold and many claw-like scratch
marks can be seen. Some are deep enough to reach through the mud and moss to the
rock surface, revealing white chalk stone underneath. This hole is absolutely
pitch black, no light seeps in at all.
name: Dark Muddy Hole
